title: US x Iran ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran announced on April 7, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between the United States and Iran, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 7 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect. 

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed two-week period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump indefinitely extended the US-Iran ceasefire on April 21 at Pakistan's request, halting airstrikes but preserving a naval blockade on Iranian ports until Tehran submits a verifiable nuclear rollback proposal and secures the Strait of Hormuz. Indirect talks mediated by Islamabad collapsed last week with US envoys withdrawing, though Iran floated a fresh three-stage plan today prioritizing de-escalation, blockade removal, and Hormuz reopening before nuclear discussions. The truce, stemming from an April 7 agreement after weeks of military escalation, remains fragile amid hardliner influence in Tehran and absent public signals from Supreme Leader Khamenei. No new summits are scheduled, but proxy flare-ups or mediator breakthroughs could prompt resumption or breakdown, sustaining oil volatility above $100 per barrel.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 99.9% implied probability of no change to the federal funds target range at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, driven by resilient economic indicators and persistent inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI surged to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while weekly jobless claims remained low at 214,000 for the period ended April 18, underscoring labor market stability with unemployment holding near 4%. March FOMC minutes highlighted elevated inflation and subdued job gains, with war-related oil shocks pushing rate cut expectations to late 2026. This positioning aligns with CME FedWatch probabilities near 100% for a hold. Realistic challenges include pre-meeting data surprises like sharper inflation deceleration or labor weakness, though traders' capital-backed sentiment shows firm conviction.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads trader consensus at 16.4% implied probability after surging to No. 1 in the latest FIFA rankings, fueled by dominant 2026 World Cup qualifiers and recent U.S. friendlies wins over Brazil (2-1) and Colombia (3-1), showcasing Mbappé's form and defensive depth as 2022 runners-up. Spain trails closely at 15.4% on Euro 2024 triumph and Nations League success, with Yamal and Rodri anchoring a youthful squad drawn into favorable Group H alongside Uruguay. England (11.1%), Argentina (8.8%), and Brazil (8.5%) keep the race tight via strong group placements—minimal early threats—and qualifier momentum, though Messi's age (39) and Brazil's rebuild introduce volatility in the expanded 48-team format's group stage and knockouts.

title: When will Bitcoin hit $150k?
description: When will Bitcoin hit $150k

markets.question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by September 30?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's commanding 3-0 first-round playoff lead over the Phoenix Suns, capped by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 42-point explosion in Game 3 on April 25, underscores their status as defending champions and top overall seed with the league's best regular-season record for the second straight year, fueling trader consensus at 51.5% implied probability amid elite defense and young core dominance from Chet Holmgren. San Antonio Spurs' 3-1 edge versus Portland after Victor Wembanyama's return from concussion and a historic Game 4 comeback on April 26 elevates them to 16.4%, reflecting their 62-win momentum and Western Conference path. Boston Celtics' 3-1 series win over Philadelphia via a 128-96 Game 4 rout positions them at 14.4% as Eastern Conference contenders, though facing tougher road ahead against Detroit or Orlando.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 27?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions California Governor Gavin Newsom as the frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 26.5% implied probability, buoyed by his executive experience managing the nation's largest state economy, strong fundraising network, and elevated national profile from prior debates and Trump-era clashes, despite California's governance critiques. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.8%, appealing to progressive and young voters through activism on economic inequality and climate policy, while former Vice President Kamala Harris sits at 8.3% following her April 10 signal of interest at the National Action Network convention—yet traders discount her after the 2024 electoral loss. The wide-open field, with no incumbent and fragmented support, could consolidate around midterm performers in swing states like Pennsylvania's Josh Shapiro or Arizona's Mark Kelly, major endorsements, or standout primary polling ahead of Iowa caucuses and New Hampshire primaries in early 2028.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no regime fall by April 30, reflecting the Iranian government's survival through a shaky ceasefire following intense U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in the early 2026 war, which weakened but did not dismantle its core institutions. Major protests from late 2025 into January—sparked by economic collapse and currency freefall—were brutally suppressed by loyal Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces, with no resurgence in the past month amid post-war stabilization efforts and diplomatic negotiations. Heightened sanctions exacerbate inflation and oil revenue shortfalls, yet security apparatus cohesion and absence of mass defections signal resilience. Only extraordinary events like a leadership vacuum or spontaneous nationwide uprising could shift odds in the final days, though historical patterns suggest low likelihood.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by violinist Linda Lampenius and pop star Pete Parkkonen has solidified its frontrunner status at 36.1% implied probability since their UMK 2026 national final victory in late February, dominating fan polls like INFE and OGAE, bookmaker odds, and predictive models amid strong crossover appeal blending classical and pop elements. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før Vi Går Hjem") leads Eurojury voting at 11.6%, buoyed by recent points tallies, while France's Monroe ("Regarde!") at 10.8% gains from similar jury support and pre-party buzz. Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") and Australia's Delta Goodrem ("Eclipse") hold steady via televote potential and internal selection hype, with Israel surging on recent momentum. First rehearsals this week in Vienna could shift dynamics ahead of semi-finals on May 12 and 14, and the May 16 grand final.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Talks between the US and Iran, mediated by Pakistan in Islamabad, have stalled amid a fragile ceasefire following US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian targets starting February 28, 2026, with both sides issuing warnings of renewed military action. Iran recently proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz and extending the truce in exchange for lifting the US naval blockade, while postponing nuclear discussions, but President Trump canceled his envoy's trip there, dimming prospects for a diplomatic breakthrough. New US sanctions on April 21 targeted Iran's missile and drone suppliers, underscoring persistent tensions over nuclear enrichment, regional proxies like Hezbollah, and security guarantees. A permanent peace deal faces steep barriers from mutual distrust and unresolved issues, with trader consensus reflecting low implied probabilities absent major concessions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2028 Republican presidential nominee favors HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49%, with Vice President J.D. Vance close behind at 39.3% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 20.8%, reflecting an open primary after President Trump's second-term limit. Recent CPAC straw polls from late March showed Vance leading at 53% and Rubio at 35%, boosted by Rubio's high-profile role in Iran diplomacy amid stalled peace talks, while Vance drew criticism for limited foreign policy involvement. RFK Jr.'s surge stems from his MAHA movement appeal and family endorsement signaling a definite run, keeping the shadow primary tight. Upcoming 2026 midterms and potential Trump endorsements could separate frontrunners.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim Polymarket edge at 19% implied probability as the 2028 presidential winner, just ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 18%, capturing trader consensus on early GOP heir apparent status and Democratic frontrunner positioning amid fragmented fields. title: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for April 27 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for 2026-04-27 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for 2026-04-27 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Royal Challengers Bengaluru hold a commanding position in second place on the IPL 2026 points table with five wins from seven matches, bolstered by Virat Kohli's consistent form and a lethal pace attack featuring Josh Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar Kumar, while Delhi Capitals sit seventh after four losses in their last five outings, including a shocking defeat to Punjab Kings on April 25 despite scoring 264 as bowlers capitulated. Arun Jaitley Stadium's pitch favors batsmen with good bounce and recent high totals like 529 runs in the prior game, making the toss crucial for setting or chasing big totals. DC miss Lungi Ngidi with Sahil Parakh debuting, and RCB lead head-to-head 20-12, heightening pressure on Axar Patel's side amid the playoffs race.

title: Israel x Hezbollah Ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 16 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed 10-day period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, effective from April 16, 2026, for an initial ten days, was extended by three weeks on April 23 following White House talks, as announced by President Donald Trump. Hezbollah was absent from negotiations but demands Israeli compliance, while both sides report mutual violations including Israeli airstrikes, home demolitions, and evacuation orders in southern Lebanon alongside Hezbollah drone incursions and rocket fire that have killed fighters. These low-level clashes since the extension underscore the truce's fragility, with Netanyahu ordering vigorous responses and upcoming potential leader summits at the White House that could drive further diplomatic progress or escalation risks.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli dominate trader consensus for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, with Russell's 39.5% implied probability edging Antonelli's 32.3% after three Grands Prix. Antonelli leads standings at 72 points to Russell's 63, propelled by back-to-back wins in China and Japan following Russell's Australia victory, highlighting Mercedes' superior pace under new regulations. The tight intra-team battle persists due to Russell's qualifying advantages, Antonelli's rapid adaptation in his second season, and a 21-race calendar with evolving energy deployment rules that could allow challengers like Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (third at 49 points) to close gaps via upgrades or mistakes.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 21 - April 28, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 21 12:00 PM ET to April 28, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Elon Musk posting 220-239 times on X from April 21-28 at 49.5% implied probability, reflecting his established pattern of 25-35 daily posts amid high engagement on cultural flashpoints like race and politics, as seen in the prior week's similar market resolving squarely in that range. A notable dip to just 14 posts per day on April 25-26—verified by X trackers—has fueled a close race with 200-219 at 31.5%, tempering upside bets while daily tallies like 33 on April 21, 43 on April 23, and 31 on April 24 underscore baseline momentum. With April 28's final window approaching, a rebound to typical volume could solidify the frontrunner, though sustained quiet risks the lower bin amid unpredictable posting bursts.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil prices have rallied to around $99 per barrel as of April 27, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on heightened geopolitical risks in the Middle East, including Strait of Hormuz tensions and Iran conflict disruptions that have slashed global supply growth forecasts to 0.6 million b/d for the year. U.S. EIA data for the week ended April 17 showed crude inventories up 1.9 million barrels against expectations of a draw, with refinery inputs dipping to 16 million b/d amid softening demand signals from China. OPEC+ countered with a modest 206,000 b/d output hike for May, aiming to ease supply strains. Traders eye the next EIA weekly report on May 1 and ongoing U.S.-Iran talks for volatility into April's close.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran diplomatic tensions persist after the first direct high-level talks since 1979 on April 11 in Islamabad, Pakistan, where US Vice President JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner met Iranian officials led by Mohammad Baqher Ghalibaf and Abbas Araghchi amid a fragile ceasefire in their ongoing conflict. Plans for a second round collapsed this week, with President Trump canceling a US delegation trip to Pakistan, prompting Iran's foreign minister to blame Washington during a visit to Russia on April 27. Tehran proposed phased negotiations—ceasefire first, Strait of Hormuz access second, nuclear issues later—but no timeline exists, leaving trader consensus focused on backchannel signals and potential escalation risks ahead of any resumption.

title: Pistons vs. Magic
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 27 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Orlando Magic hold a 2-1 lead over the top-seeded Detroit Pistons in their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series, with Game 4 set for Amway Center on April 27. The Magic's dramatic 113-105 Game 3 comeback from a 17-point deficit—fueled by 25 points each from Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane—shifted momentum after Detroit evened the series with a dominant 98-83 Game 2 win. Orlando faces challenges with key absences on the injury report: Franz Wagner (ankle), Jonathan Isaac (knee), Jett Howard (ankle), and Anthony Black (abdomen) all out, thinning their depth against a healthy Pistons squad boasting a league-best 60-22 regular-season record. Detroit's physicality and home-court advantage in Games 1-2 underscore their resilience, while Magic home cooking and playoff urgency could extend the series.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United enter their Old Trafford clash with Brentford as slim trader favorites at 48.5% implied probability, reflecting home advantage and momentum from a recent Chelsea victory, though a lingering center-back crisis tempers enthusiasm. Harry Maguire returns from suspension to bolster the defense, but Lisandro Martínez remains banned, Matthijs de Ligt sidelined by a back injury, and Leny Yoro nursing a knock, forcing makeshift pairings like Ayden Heaven or Noussair Mazraoui at center-back. Brentford, priced at 26.5% with draw at 25.5%, face their own absences—Fabio Carvalho (knee), Vitaly Janelt (ankle), Rico Henry (thigh)—yet boast a competitive head-to-head record, including a 3-1 win over United last September, keeping the matchup tightly contested in the Premier League table chase.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 25 - April 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 25 12:00 PM ET to April 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ninth-seeded Mirra Andreeva enters her WTA Mutua Madrid Open round of 16 matchup against Anna Bondar as trader consensus favorite, propelled by her world No. 8 ranking and tour-leading clay-court wins in 2026—including a Linz title and straight-sets triumphs over Panna Udvardy and Dalma Galfi this week on the high-bouncing Caja Magica courts. Bondar, ranked 63rd, fuels upset potential with a strong run, stunning seventh seed Elina Svitolina 6-3, 6-4 before rallying past Laura Samson 7-6(3), 6-1 yesterday. Bondar holds a 1-0 head-to-head edge from their 2023 Lausanne clay encounter. No reported injuries or weather disruptions expected.

title: LoL: DN SOOPers Challengers vs Gen.G Global Academy (BO3) - LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between DN SOOPers Challengers and Gen.G Global Academy in the LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 27 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "DN SOOPers Challengers" if DN SOOPers Challengers win the match against Gen.G Global Academy.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G Global Academy" if Gen.G Global Academy win the match against DN SOOPers Challengers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 3. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both DN SOOPers Challengers and Gen.G Global Academy each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both DN SOOPers Challengers and Gen.G Global Academy each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: DN SOOPers Challengers hold a mid-table edge at 6th in LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2 standings with a 4-4 series record and 10-12 map differential, positioning them just above the Trial Group cutoff in the double round-robin BO3 format. Gen.G Global Academy languishes in 9th at 2-6 series (7-13 maps), hampered by recent sweeps including 0-2 losses to Nongshim Esports Academy and KT Rolster Challengers in Week 4. DNS.C snapped a skid with a 2-1 win over Hanwha Life Esports Challengers last week but fell 1-2 to NS.EA, showing mixed Blue/Red side form under Patch 26.08. Stable rosters for both—DNS.C's Lancer/DDoiV/Flip/Enosh/Peter vs. GGA's Ripple/Courage/Kemish/MUDai/Lumos—highlight DNS.C's momentum in this pivotal matchup impacting Challenge Group advancement.

title: Trump Today: April 25
description: This is a market over what Donald Trump will do on April 25, 2026.

markets.question: Will Trump dance today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump dances between 12:00AM ET and 11:59PM ET on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Dancing" is defined as deliberate, rhythmic body movement typically matched music or a beat, such as swaying, stepping, or coordinated hand or body motions. Casual gesturing, clapping, or incidental body movement will not qualify. Dancing without the presence of music or a beat will qualify.

AI-generated content, deepfakes, or altered footage will not be considered. 

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

This market will resolve based on video.

markets.question: Will Trump wear a red tie today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump wears a red tie at any point on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The color will be determined by the most visually dominant color visible in publicly available photos or videos. For example, for the tie to be considered predominantly blue, its primary color must be a shade of blue, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. This tie would count as "Blue": https://i.ytimg.com/vi/20w7J2JAmqw/maxresdefault.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "No". https://www.usatoday.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2025/01/20/USAT/77837111007-xxx-dcmg-105-5190802.jpg

If Donald Trump does not wear a tie or if no photos or video are released from the listed date, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump talk to Xi Jinping today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump sign an executive order today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve "Yes" if Donald Trump signs an executive order on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Executive actions will not qualify toward this market’s resolution.

This market will immediately resolve "Yes" if the text of an executive order for the given day is published on the White House page for presidential actions (https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/) or the White House press pool. Mere announcements will not qualify.

If no executive order is published by 12:00 PM ET the day after the listed date of this market, this market will resolve to “No”.

In the case of ambiguity this market may remain open until it is confirmed by the federal register.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Sleepy Joe" today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner on April 25, 2026, disrupted President Trump's public appearance at the Washington Hilton, prompting evacuation by Secret Service alongside the First Lady and Vice President JD Vance after shots near security; suspect Cole Thomas Allen was arrested with no injuries reported. Trump delivered brief safety remarks and shared surveillance footage on Truth Social confirming senior officials unharmed, while no executive order was published on whitehouse.gov that day. Trader consensus reflects evidentiary disputes over specific actions like dancing or attire, pending UMA oracle review for resolution based on credible video within the 24-hour window, amid high volume exceeding $650,000. Upcoming oracle decision and potential event rescheduling could clarify outcomes.

title: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's second term, underway since January 20, 2025 and set to conclude January 20, 2029, faces no active threats to his tenure by April 30, just days away, driving trader consensus to 99.9% on "No." Constitutional removal paths—impeachment requiring House passage and two-thirds Senate conviction, voluntary resignation, or 25th Amendment invocation by the vice president and cabinet—demand weeks or months, with no recent legislative votes, official announcements, or credible reporting on health crises, scandals, or internal administration discord in the past 30 days to catalyze action. While unpredictable events like a sudden medical emergency or late-breaking legal ruling could theoretically shift dynamics, the compressed timeline and procedural barriers underpin high-confidence pricing among real-money traders.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves hold a commanding 3-1 series lead over the Nuggets in the Western Conference first-round playoffs heading into Game 5 at Denver's Ball Arena, where the host Nuggets face elimination and leverage home-court advantage. Minnesota's decisive 112-96 Game 4 victory featured Ayo Dosunmu's explosive 43-point bench outburst, but came at a steep cost: Anthony Edwards ruled out with a left knee bone bruise and hyperextension expected to sideline him multiple weeks, plus Donte DiVincenzo lost to a torn right Achilles early in that contest. Denver's Aaron Gordon remains questionable with lingering left calf tightness after a limited Game 4, while Peyton Watson stays sidelined by hamstring issues; an NBA probe into post-Game 4 scuffle antics could yield fines but no reported suspensions yet. Trader consensus reflects Nuggets' desperation edge against a hobbled Wolves roster, though Minnesota's depth has proven resilient amid the chaos.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cagliari's explosive start with Paul Mendy's brace in the opening 8 minutes on dream debut has propelled trader consensus to price their Serie A win at Unipol Domus at 84.5% implied probability against Atalanta, surging after regaining a 3-2 lead via Gennaro Borrelli's 47th-minute strike despite Gianluca Scamacca's late first-half equalizer. Atalanta, seventh in standings with a +16 goal difference from 33 matches, trail at 1.1% comeback odds with under 15 minutes remaining, hampered by Lorenzo Bernasconi's season-ending knee injury and midweek Coppa Italia fatigue. Cagliari, 16th and relegation-threatened post a 3-0 loss to Inter, leverage home form amid their long-term absences like Pavoletti and Mazzitelli, underscoring the match's high stakes and rapid sentiment shift.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid escalating US-Iran conflict, Kharg Island—Iran's vital Persian Gulf oil export hub handling 90% of crude shipments—remains under firm Iranian control, as affirmed by a parliamentary security spokesman visiting the site on April 27, reporting normal operations despite reinforced defenses. US airstrikes on April 7 targeted over 50 military sites including missile storage and radar, sparing oil infrastructure per Iranian assessments, while a naval blockade of Iranian ports continues post-ceasefire collapse. President Trump has reiterated threats of ground troops to seize the island for leverage in Strait of Hormuz negotiations and peace talks, with US officials signaling possible assaults to allies. Traders eye diplomatic proposals or further military escalation as key catalysts.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 99.7% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz commercial traffic normalizing by April 30, reflecting skin-in-the-game conviction amid the US-Iran naval standoff enforcing a mutual blockade now in its eighth week. Ship-tracking data shows transits at 1-5 vessels daily—under 10% of pre-crisis volumes—with Reuters confirming as of April 27 that oil tankers are being turned back, perpetuating the virtual standstill despite fleeting ceasefire attempts. This dynamic has sustained elevated oil volatility and rerouting costs for Gulf exporters. Tail risks include a surprise diplomatic breakthrough or multilateral intervention in the final days, though persistent mutual seizures signal low odds of rapid de-escalation.
